facts.text: Phoenix was settled in 1867 as an agricultural community near the Salt and Gila Rivers and incorporated as a city in 1881.
The city’s first NBA franchise, the Phoenix Suns, was awarded in 1968 and played at the Coliseum before moving to America West Arena in 1992.
In 2008, the city’s second‑tallest peak, Squaw Peak, was renamed Piestewa Peak to honor Army Specialist Lori Ann Piestewa, the first Native American woman killed in combat.
Numerous Hollywood films and television shows have been shot in Phoenix, making the city a frequent backdrop for major feature productions.
Archaeological evidence suggests that droughts and floods between 1300 and 1450 led the Hohokam civilization to abandon the Phoenix area.
